Run 178: ACTIVE INTERVENTION — memory crisis. Available RAM was 539Mi (down from 2.7Gi). Root cause: 10 orphaned chrome-devtools-mcp processes (ppid=1) from old pipeline cycles, each keeping a Chrome browser alive. Total Chrome RSS ~10GB. Killed all 10 orphans — memory recovered to 5.3Gi. Fixed cron-orchestrator.sh: added cleanup block at cycle start to kill orphaned MCPs and remove stale Puppeteer profiles. Also cleaned up 11 stale /tmp/puppeteer_dev_chrome_profile-* dirs.
Run 177: Monitoring run. 14:00 cycle in progress. SYSTEM CRITICAL confirmed VERIFIED by tester. Supervisor verified 0 console errors via chrome-devtools.

concerns.note: PM agent blindly reverts FAILED tasks without reading tester's actual issue report. TASK-082 was reverted using a stale note about measure.js when the real failure was a different bug (thumbnail selector mismatch). If this recurs, PM prompt needs a rule: always read tester Issues section before changing FAILED status.

tasks.md at 185KB/391 lines (100KB threshold). Growing ~8KB/cycle. 32 DONE tasks with multi-KB verbose descriptions. Tester throughput 1/cycle vs 2 developers. DONE backlog will keep growing. Options: (1) trim verbose DONE descriptions, (2) batch-verify simple tasks, (3) slow idea generation.

Stale puppeteer profiles accumulating despite orchestrator cleanup (40 found this run). Orchestrator cleanup may not be catching all profiles — possibly from supervisor or other non-pipeline chrome sessions.
